overview: The Village of Weston Parks Committee approved installation of new kayak launch signs for the Plover River water trail and deferred discussion on allowing dogs at the aquatic center until next month when the absent chair can participate. The committee also received updates on Kennedy Park fundraising efforts and discussed graffiti removal challenges on the Oaklair River Trail.
presiding: Not explicitly named, but acting chair conducted the meeting in absence of Katrina

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Yellow Banks disc golf course update
Dog day at the aquatic center
Graffiti on the Oaklair River Trail
Topics for future meetings and staff remarks

discussions.body: Minutes from the April 27, 2026 meeting were approved. A motion was made and seconded with no discussion. The motion carried unanimously.

Director Sean Osterbrink presented a timeline of the Kennedy Park master plan process as requested by the board, covering from the 2023 comprehensive outdoor recreation plan through the May 9th ribbon cutting. Scott asked clarifying questions about village capital funds and fundraising timelines. Osterbrink explained the Friends of Kennedy Park are a volunteer nonprofit currently fundraising for lights on the upper field, having approximately $84-85,000 at the community foundation toward a $117,000 goal. The acting chair defended the Friends group, noting they plan to announce fundraising totals publicly at their June tournament and deserve grace as volunteers.

discussions.item: Yellow Banks disc golf course update
discussions.body: Staff presented photos and a PDF showing locations where fallen trees were removed from the river and details of wood mat installation over gas pipelines. Dan explained the mats were used to protect TC Energy (formerly ANR/ATC) pipelines during equipment access. The village now owns the mats with extras potentially available for other trail projects.

discussions.item: Dog day at the aquatic center
discussions.body: The committee deferred this item to next month because Chair Katrina, who had requested the discussion, was absent due to an unforeseen issue. A resident named Andy who attended specifically to oppose allowing dogs at the public pool stated his objection: 'There should not be animals allowed into a public pool. Period. I like dogs, but it's not a place for them. We have rivers. We have lakes. That's where they belong.' He indicated he may return for next month's discussion.

discussions.item: Graffiti on the Oaklair River Trail
discussions.body: Staff discussed challenges removing graffiti from concrete and benches along the trail. Access limitations prevent bringing pressure washing equipment to the site. Options discussed included anti-vandalism coatings, seal coats, and volunteer work days. Committee members suggested community volunteers might help with maintenance, similar to how directors previously installed benches during a team-building day. No formal action was taken; staff will research cleaning options.

Dan from Marathon County Parks presented version 7 of informational signs for Plover River kayak launches. Three signs will be installed in Weston (Highway J, Ross Avenue, and Yellow Banks). Signs include maps, QR codes linking to the Kelly Station river gauge, and donation information for Marathon County Parks Foundation which funded the project through state grants. Dan noted the Highway J launch may be closed all season due to bridge replacement. Motion to approve sign installation passed unanimously.

Osterbrink reported dugout painting at Kennedy Park was completed, aquatic center pool repairs were finished, and pool filling began with opening day scheduled for June 6th. He also announced the Mock Miller Park master plan process will begin with a kickoff meeting with Rettler next Tuesday. The last open house for the bike and pedestrian plan is June 11th from 5-7pm.

publicComment: Two public comments were offered. Andy (6504 [street inaudible]) spoke in person opposing dogs at the aquatic center, stating animals should not be allowed in public pools. Jim Pensan (5002 [street] Weston), speaking online under the name 'Vern M,' criticized the 4pm meeting time as discouraging public participation and questioned Kennedy Park fundraising transparency, calling the $8,700 expenditure figure 'too coincidental' and asking about the location of grant money and glow event proceeds. He suggested the Friends of Kennedy Park may be 'frauds' for not sharing detailed financials.
actionItems: Dog day at aquatic center discussion deferred to June meeting
Yellow Banks kayak launch signs approved for installation at three Weston locations
Staff to research graffiti removal and anti-vandalism coating options for Oaklair River Trail
Staff to bring food allergy awareness playground signage proposal to future meeting
Mock Miller Park master plan kickoff meeting with Rettler scheduled for next Tuesday
Next Parks Committee meeting scheduled for June 22, 2026
title: Parks Committee
